campaigns.title: Help visually impaired Shivaprakash give 40 blind girls shelter, education and care
campaigns.description: At the age of 8, Shivaprakash lost his vision. But what could have become a life of despair became something else entirely. Shivaprakash went on to complete his Master’s degree and eventually founded the Divya Jyothi Charitable Trust, an organisation born out of personal struggle and driven by a powerful mission, to educate and empower blind girls, who are among the most neglected and marginalised in society.In many families, blind daughters are hidden away, seen as a burden, and in some cases, even as a curse. They are denied the right to dream, grow, or be independent. At Divya Jyothi, Shivaprakash is changing that narrative. Your support can ensure that no blind girl is left behind simply because of her disability. Donate today to help them stand tall, proud, and self-reliant. Donate nowDonate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).Education is survivalIn communities where gender and disability intersect, blind girls face deep-rooted prejudice and extreme isolation. Many are denied even basic schooling and are left dependent on family members for the rest of their lives. Recognising this, Divya Jyothi began with a simple yet radical step: to provide blind girls with computer training so they could access the digital world and open doors to employment.But the journey didn’t stop there. The NGO went on to launch a braille transcription centre, ensuring girls have access to learning materials that suit their needs. As Shivaprakash says, “Without education, no one can do anything. That's why education is even more important for blind girls.” Education isn’t just about literacy, it’s about self-worth, decision-making, mobility, and freedom. It’s about giving these girls the same right to participate in society that sighted individuals often take for granted.From rejection to respectIn a country where blind girls are often treated as liabilities, Divya Jyothi is rewriting their futures. Over 1,000 girls have graduated from the Trust’s training centres. Many of them come from families that had almost given up on them. Today, these same girls are government employees, corporate professionals, and role models in their communities.More than 200 graduates have secured jobs in the public sector. Over 300 are now employed in private companies. Every success story is proof that with the right support, blind girls don’t just survive, they thrive. “Most girls are neglected by their families. This is a major challenge for them,” says Shivaprakash. By providing them with skills and confidence, Divya Jyothi is turning neglect into empowerment and helplessness into hope.Donate to help 40 blind girls find independenceAlmost 40 girls are currently enrolled and will directly benefit from your support. These are girls with potential, courage, and ambition—but they lack the resources to pursue an independent life. “I request each one to come forward and donate to help blind girls lead independent lives in society,” urges Shivaprakash.Help Divya Jyothi Charitable Trust break the cycle of neglect. Your donation can give a blind girl her first lesson, her first job, and a life of dignity.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s most trusted online donation platform, to support Divya Jyothi Charitable Trust in its mission to shelter and educate neglected and abandoned blind girls.

campaigns.title: Help Girish give 250 children, rescued from abuse in red-light areas a new life
campaigns.description: In the dark and dingy alleys of red light areas, injustice, pain and suffering run rampant. And the worst affected are the children who grow up in red-light areas and are subjected to constant abuse and violence. “The sight of a 12-year-old girl being gang-raped by a group of men near a house l haunts me to this date. I was filled with anguish and resolved to do something,” says Girish Kulkarni who has made it his life’s mission to rescue children from red light areas and give them a safe and secure childhood. In the last 3 decades Girish has saved over 1100 girls from a life of abuse in red-light areas, giving them a loving home, education and medical care. He has further supported over 20,000 children with a better life.Sadly, Of the 250 the children rescued from abuse and exploitation, around 70 of them are HIV positive. The high cost of medication has left Girish struggling for funds. Your support can help him provide these children with the care and upbringing they need. Donate now.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India), & 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code (USA). A movement to protect vulnerable children“Growing up in Ahilyanagar, the experiences I had as a child witnessing the horrors of sex trade defined the path of my life. I had even seen my friend’s 15-year-old sister and 70-year-old grandmother being forced to do sex work for the price of a cup of tea”, recalls Girish. “I started by merely feeding and teaching children of sex workers initially with the intention of giving them some respite from the horrors of life of abuse in red-light areas. Gradually more and more mothers started coming to me and asked me to take their children away, even if it was just for a couple of hours,” he further adds.The work that Girish started in his youth gradually grew to become a movement, with many more joining his cause. His NGO Snehalaya to date has transformed the lives of millions. Girish’s work is crucial for the survival and safety of vulnerable children of sex workers. Snehalaya is almost entirely dependent on donations from the public and your support can strengthen his mission to save children most in need. Donate generously.The horrors of life in red light areas“The cruelty and abuse that children as young as 6 or 7 years of age face is something that I can’t explain in words. Young daughters of sex workers are made to watch their mothers being raped, are brutally assaulted and forced into sex work. No child deserves to grow like this, Girish says in anguish.Sad reality of life for children growing up in red light areas is that they are deprived of even the most basic needs like proper food, safety and education. Moreover, a good number of children rescued by Girish are diagnosed HIV positive. The task of securing medication, providing them quality education and much-needed nutrition is one that requires constant support from donors. Moreover, the increase in healthcare cost has left Girish struggling to keep his home for children going. But your support can enable him to give his children a loving environment and save many more who still remain trapped behind closed doors.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest and most trusted online donation platform, to help Snehalaya give hundreds of children rescued from red-light areas a life away from violence and abuse.

campaigns.title: Help Leena in saving girls from being lost forever in red-light areas
campaigns.description: "Hundreds of young girls are lost forever in the dark lanes of red-light areas," says Leena Kejriwal, the founder of Missing Link Trust. These girls endure unimaginable abuse and exploitation, being lured or trafficked into an industry that breaks them physically and emotionally. Missing Link Trust is on a mission to prevent the tragedy before it happens, and also rehabilitate girls and women who are rescued from forced sex work.Leena’s journey began with her powerful street art project in Kolkata, where she painted haunting silhouettes of missing girls to raise awareness against sex trafficking. Now, she leads a larger fight — one that focuses on prevention and rehabilitation. But this mission needs your support.You can save countless girl children from going missing forever. Donate now.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).Preventing Trafficking Before It Begins“When girls come into a red-light area, the first thing is that they are broken, physically and emotionally,” Leena says. “It’s like they will be caught, hands and legs tied, and all heinous crimes committed on them.” These are the brutal realities that Missing Link Trust fights against every day.Many of these girls are from impoverished regions like the Sundarbans, and traffickers prey on their vulnerability. “It’s shocking that they sell themselves for as low as a hundred rupees,” Leena adds. "They ready her through violent acts, removing all threads of shame from her."But why wait until a girl is trafficked to save her? Missing Link Trust believes in stopping trafficking at its root — through “prevention, prevention, prevention” as Leena puts it. The goal is simple: protect girls from ever being lost to this darkness.A Safe World for Children“Our mission is the prevention of abuse and exploitation of children,” Leena emphasizes. “Our vision is to create a safe world for children, and we want to do this with a preventive methodology.”Every Donation MattersYour support will help Missing Link Trust continue its vital work of rescuing, rehabilitating, and preventing the trafficking of young girls. The funds raised will go directly towards:Prevention programs in vulnerable areas to stop trafficking before it happens.Rehabilitation services for girls rescued from red-light areas, providing them with medical care, emotional support, and education.Awareness campaigns to reach at-risk communities and ensure girls are protected from traffickers.Together, We Can Make a Difference“Why wait for a girl to get trafficked to save her?” asks Leena. With your help, we can stop this horror before it begins. Your donation will help us create a world where no girl is lost to the nightmare of trafficking.Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest and most trusted online giving platform, to support Missing Link Trust to save young girls from being trafficked and help rehabilitate those rescued.

campaigns.title: Help Chitra save disabled children from poor families from a life of neglect and abuse
campaigns.description: Chitra Shah noticed that a patient with disabilities had missed several of her appointments with a psychologist, where she worked. Determined to find out why, Chitra went to the girl’s house which was located near a garbage dump, and she found the house locked. But she kept hearing noises from within the house. Looking inside, she found the missing patient tied to a chair and covered in her own urine and waste. The patient was a young girl with Down’s Syndrome and because of her disability; she was locked up every day and suffered in silence. This incident led Chitra Shah to establish Satya Special School in 2003. Since then, she has provided thousands of special needs children with therapies, education and vocational skills training to live their lives with dignity and respect. Many children with disabilities are abandoned because of their disabilities. Satya Special School believes in empowering disabled children and helping them stand on their own independently so they never have to be shunned, humiliated and abused because of their disabilities. Your support can help children with disabilities receive therapy and education. Donate now.Donate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India), & 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code (USA). Place of rehabilitation and transformation For 20 years, Satya Special School has been working with special-needs children in 10 centres located in rural and urban Puducherry. The centres have trained teachers and physical therapists who work with the children to identify their special needs and develop physical and educational programs to help them be integrated into mainstream life. The children are provided with multiple therapies such as physiotherapy, hydrotherapy, speech therapy, art therapy and more to improve their physical condition and help them function independently. The school also provides the children with specialized educational support to help them integrate into mainstream schools. This School Readiness Program includes one-on-one special education, activity-based learning, visual media learning and more. Satya Special School is also equipped with facilities such as a sensory walkway, an outdoor inclusive disabled-friendly play park, a wheelchair accessible vehicle and many more facilities to assist children with physical disabilities.Need for the fundraiserThe work of Satya Special School is making an impact in the lives of the children, many of whom have learned to function independently, been enrolled in mainstream schools and secured employment. But the school is in desperate need of funds to afford rehabilitation equipment such as walkers and standing frames, custom made shoes for people with disabilities and other therapeutic interventions. Your support will help the special needs children continue to have access to the tools, resources and therapies that make their lives better and give them a chance to have self-reliant and hopeful lives.The parents and guardians of the children were previously in despair regarding their children’s helplessness but because of Satya Special School, they are watching their children grow into independent and capable individuals who work towards their dreams for the future. The stigma, discrimination and abuse of disabled children is widespread, and many special-needs children are abandoned by their own families. But ”we transform their lives through building their abilities” , shares Chitra Shah, the founder of Satya Special School. Donate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest online donation platform, to support Satya Special School in their mission to provide multiple therapies, rehabilitation, academic support and future job readiness skills to 61 special needs children.

campaigns.title: Help Sandip rescue and care for abandoned elderly left to beg on the streets. Donate now
campaigns.description: “I never imagined I would have to sleep at the bus stand despite having an able-bodied son. I was a building contractor, and it was all fine till the time I was earning well. But as soon as I lost my leg in an accident, I came on the streets. My son assaulted me and deserted me,” recalls Laxman Parasram Ajrekar, a beneficiary of Jeevan Anand Sanstha’s home for elderly, a sanctuary for over 230 seniors like him.Over the last decade Sandip Parab and his NGO Jeevan Anand Sanstha, despite facing financial hardships himself, have been a saviour for over 3,000 elderly.  With your support, Sandip can continue to rescue the elderly parents who are abandoned by their own children and give them the love and care they deserve. Donate nowDonate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India), & 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code (USA). A fate worse than death“Just imagine the plight of an elderly mother who, despite coming from a well-to-do background is compelled to live the life of a beggar on city streets. The condition of elderly women who have been abandoned by their children is the worst because they face so many problems and threats on the streets. They lie starving on the streets with festering wounds and incapable of even moving,” says Sandip while talking about the pain of homeless seniors. Sandip has made it his life’s mission to protect and care for such senior citizens and has resolved never to rest till he can reach every elderly who is in need. The only hurdle Sandip faces is that of funds. Your support can enable him to reach countless more senior citizens who have been abused and abandoned by their families.How your donations helpSuch has been Sandip’s resolve to save the elderly from the streets that he has been rescuing them on his two wheeler. Today he faces a huge challenge in providing rescued senior citizens nutrition and medical care. “I treat each and every person at our home for elderly as my own parents. I do everything for them from bathing and cleaning to feeding them. But the problem I am facing is that the number of elderly at our home is increasing with each passing day. I need support in getting rations and medicines for this family of mine” shares Sandip.“When I was in my 20s, I found an elderly person lying unconscious near a railway station. I took him to my friend's home, cleaned him, bathed him and began taking care of him. I later found out that he was the principal of a school. His son was a professor but despite that he had been lying on the road. It is for helpless seniors like these that I decided that I will dedicate my life to care for them.Join this mission to protect vulnerable and helpless senior citizens who are in urgent need of medical care, food and shelter. Donate nowDonate to this fundraiser by Give, India’s largest online donation platform, to support Jeevan Anand Sansthan and other NGOs working in this cause to further their mission of rescuing abandoned elderly from the streets and giving them a loving home.

campaigns.title: Help Vinay bring children facing abuse in red-light areas to safety and protect them
campaigns.description: “I saw a girl, barely 7 or 8, being pushed into flesh trade. Her cries for help still echo in my ears. There were men as old as 60 r*ping the innocent child. That was the day I knew my mission in life was to get these children out of the hell-like conditions in red-light areas. They are nothing short of hell and no child deserves to grow up in this environment” says Vinay Vasta, founder of Social Activities Integration, an organisation committed to freeing children trapped in red-light areas.Vinay and his NGO have been able to provide education, medical care and nutrition to over 2,500 children. But his dream to provide safe shelter to children remains unrealized. “We do our best to keep these children safe during the day but sadly they are forced to go back to the red light areas at night as we don’t have a home for them. Your donation can help us build a safe home for children of sex workers and prevent them from being pulled into prostitution. Donate to make this dream a realityDonate easily through UPI, all debit/credit cards and net banking. Charitable donations to this fundraising campaign are tax exempted under 80G of the Income Tax Act (India).Life in red light areas“S*x workers use their homes which are barely 6 feet by 4 feet for their business. They invite customers in the presence of their children. Their children not only witness abuse but experience it too. Just imagine a child being told that all she can ever become in life is a pr*stitute”, Vinay shares as he explains the extent of abuse children face in red light areas.This is a life that thousands of children are forced to live behind closed doors where they suffer abuse and exploitation morning, noon and night.A ray of hopeVinay has been relentlessly trying to give children the care and education they deserve. He has provided quality English education to thousands of children, enabling them to secure a livelihood out of red-light areas. Several children taught by Vinay are today leading successful lives as professionals and Government employees. However the sad reality is that Vinay and his NGO, in the absence of a permanent shelter home, are unable to provide round the clock safety to these children, which is essential for their well-being.Your donations can enable SAI to build a shelter home for the children of sex workers and usher in a new tomorrow for them, away from the horrors of red-light areas.Donate to this fundraiser on Give.do, India’s largest and most trusted online donation platform, to provide safe shelter for children of sex workers and save them from abuse and second generation pr*stitution.NGO's 80G number: AACTS0408EF20216NGO's 12A number: AACTS0408EE20028

campaigns.title: Football and Life Skills Programme - Education With A Kick
campaigns.description: OSCAR Foundation is one of the leading grassroots Sports for Development (S4D) organisations in India. It uses the power of football to encourage children to stay in school and develop essential life skills, making them resilient and future ready.Since 2010, we have empowered over 25,000 children and youth in low-income communities, providing them with the necessary tools to become role models in their communities.Our "8 to 18 Football Life Skills" programme aims to provide critical life skills to children, enabling them to not only survive but thrive. By offering mentorship at an early age and creating a fun and safe environment, OSCAR seeks to develop resilient communities where children and youth can become successful role models.Children in slums and rural areas face significant challenges—caste, gender and poverty often prevent them from reaching their full potential. In India, over 20% of students dropout of school before completing 10th grade, and mental health issues are rising among youth. We believe every child deserves a chance to succeed.Your contribution will directly support our efforts to break the cycle of poverty and create resilient communities. By funding our program, you help us provide critical mentorship and life skills to children who need it most.

campaigns.title: Help abandoned elderly with shelter and care
campaigns.description: Every day, elderly parents are left alone on the streets, at train stations, or even outside temples. Many are sick, struggling with age-related problems, and have no money or family to turn to. Some suffer for weeks or months before anyone notices their pain. The heartbreaking truth? They were abandoned by their own families.Although Indian law requires children and relatives to care for senior citizens, many elders still face neglect, abuse, and abandonment. The problem is only getting worse.But there is hope. Our dedicated NGO partners work day and night to rescue these forgotten elders. They provide safe shelter, medical care, nutritious meals, and most importantly—love and respect. Through old age homes across the country, our partners give these seniors a chance to live their remaining years with dignity, comfort, and peace.You can make a difference. Your donation will help our NGO partners:Rescue homeless elders from the streetsProvide shelter, food, and medical careRestore their faith in humanity with kindness and supportNo elder should spend their final years alone and suffering. Your generosity can give them a fresh start. Donate today and help an abandoned senior citizen find safety, care, and hope.
